ddls:
CREATE TABLE "governors" (
  "governor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each governor or trustee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the governor or trustee */,
  "appointed_by" TEXT /* Body or entity that appointed the governor or trustee */,
  "appointed_from" TEXT /* Start date of the appointment term */,
  "appointed_to" TEXT /* End date of the appointment term */,
  "appointment_type" TEXT /* Type of appointment (elected or nominated) */,
  "position" TEXT /* Specific role or position within the governing body */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for communication */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number for communication */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the governor or trustee */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the appointment (active, resigned, suspended) */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the governor or trustee */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief biography of the governor or trustee */,
  "photo_url" TEXT /* URL of the governor or trustee's photo */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("governor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "email" TEXT /* User's email address */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the roles table */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the roles table */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the permissions table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communications" (
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication */,
  "sender_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the users table (sender) */,
  "receiver_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the users table (receiver) */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the communication */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the communication */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the communication was sent */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the communication has been read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("communication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documents" (
  "document_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the document */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the document */,
  "file_url" TEXT /* URL of the document file */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the users table (uploader) */,
  "uploaded_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the document was uploaded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("document_id")
)
